30.8: Put vanity and the word of a lie far away from me; do not give poverty or wealth to me; provide me the bread of my portion,
30.9: lest I become satisfied and I deny and say, ‘Who is YHWH?’ And lest I become dispossessed and steal, and I seize the name of my God.
30.10: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you and you be found guilty.
30.11: A generation lightly esteems its father, and does not bless its mother.
30.12: A generation is pure in its own eyes and yet has not been washed from its filth.
30.13: A generation—how high its eyes, and its eyelids are lifted up!
30.14: A generation—its teeth are swords and its fangs are knives, to devour the poor from the earth, and needy ones from mankind.
30.15: Unto the leech are two daughters—‘Give, and Give. Three things are never satisfied, four never say, ‘Enough!’:
30.16: Sheol, and the barren womb, earth not satisfied with water, and the fire never having said, ‘Enough!’
30.17: The eye that mocks at a father and despises obedience to a mother—the ravens of the valley will dig it out, and the young eagles will eat it.
30.18: Three things , they are wonderful for me, and four, I cannot know them:
30.19: The way of an eagle in the heavens, the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship in the midst of the sea, and the way of a man with a maiden.
30.20: This is the way of a woman committing adultery: She eats and has wiped her mouth and has said, ‘I have not done wickedness.’
30.21: For three things the earth has trembled, and for four it is not able to bear up:
30.22: Under a servant when he becomes king, and a fool when he is filled with food,
30.23: under a hated woman when she is married, and a maidservant when she dispossesses her mistress.
30.24: Four things , they are small on the earth; they are wiser than wise:
30.25: The ants are a people not strong, and they prepare their food in the summer;
30.26: the rock badgers are a people not strong, and they set their home in the cliffs;
30.27: a king is not unto the locust, and go they forth in formation, all of them;
30.28: the lizard can be caught in one’s hands, and it is in the palaces of king.
30.29: Three things , they are are doing well of step, even four are doing well in the walk:
30.30: A lion, mighty among the beasts, and it does not turn back from the face of anything;
30.31: one girded of loins, or a he-goat, and a king with his army around him.
30.32: If you have been foolish in lifting up yourself, and if you have schemed—hand to mouth!
30.33: For as the churning of milk brings forth butter, and pressing of the nose brings forth blood, so the pressing of anger brings forth strife.”

31.1: The words of Lemuel the king—the burden that his mother taught him:
31.2: What, O my son? And what, O son of my womb? And what, O son of my vows?
31.3: Do not give your strength to the women, and your ways to wiping away kings.
31.4: Not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor for rulers strong drink,
31.5: lest they drink and forget the decree, and pervert the judgment of any of the sons of affliction.
31.6: Give strong drink to the one perishing, and wine to those bitter of soul.
31.7: Let him drink and forget his poverty, and not remember his trouble anymore.
31.8: Open your mouth for a mute one, for the cause of all the sons of destitution.
31.9: Open your mouth, judge with righteousness, and plead the cause of the poor one and needy one.
31.10: A wife of worth, who can find her ? And far above rubies is her value.
31.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he is not lacking in spoil.
31.12: She has done him good, and not evil, all the days of her life.
31.13: She has sought wool and flax, and she works it with the delight of her palms.
31.14: She is like the ships of a merchant—from afar she brings in her bread.
31.15: And she rises and it is still night, and she provides food for her house, even the portion assigned to her maidservants.
31.16: She considers a field and buys it; from the fruit of her palms she plants a vineyard.
31.17: She girds her loins with strength and strengthens her arms.
31.18: She tastes that her profit is good; her lamp is not extinguished in the night.
31.19: She stretched out her hands to the distaff, and her palms grasp the spindle.
31.20: Her palm she spreads out to a poor one, and her hands she sends out to a needy one.
31.21: She does not fear for her house because of snow, for all her house is clothed in scarlet.
31.22: Coverings she has made for herself; fine linen and purple are her clothing.
31.23: Her husband is known in the gates, in his sitting with the elders of the land.
31.24: Linen garments she has made and sold, and a girdle she gives to the merchant.
31.25: Strength and honor are her clothing, and she laughs at the day to come.
31.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and instruction of kindness is on her tongue.
31.27: She watches the goings of her house, and the bread of idleness she does not eat.
31.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed, and her husband he praises her:
31.29: “Many daughters have acted with worth, and you have gone up over them all.”
31.30: Charm is deceptive and beauty is vapor, and a woman fearing YHWH—she is to be praised.
31.31: Give to her from the fruit of her hands, and let her own deeds praise her in the gates.
